name: Mijnlieff
author: Andy Hopwood (2010), Hopwood Games / AGP
description: Mijnlieff (Andy Hopwood, 2010 — UK Games Expo Best Abstract Game). A 4x4 tile-placement duel where every tile you play dictates where your OPPONENT must play next: Straights force the same row/column, Diagonals the same diagonal, Pullers a touching square, Pushers a non-touching square. Each player has 8 tiles (two of each type); the first tile must go on an edge square. If you can't play you pass and your opponent places freely. When a player places their last tile the opponent gets ONE final placement. Score 1 point per line of 3 in your colour (a line of 4 = 2); highest score wins.
